title: campanula rotundifolia subsp. stenocodon
intro: Campanula rotundifolia subsp. stenocodon, commonly known as the Scottish harebell or Scottish bluebell, is a plant species belonging to the family Campanulaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe and calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress, as well as its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
